description: Backfilling all history at once without throttling or idempotency, overwhelming the warehouse or overwriting partial data.
body: Failure mode: a backfill reprocesses the entire history in one unbounded job, starving the live pipeline and warehouse, and if the transform is not idempotent it overwrites partitions with partial results. Detection signals: live pipelines miss SLAs during the backfill; warehouse slots/queues saturate; partitions end up with fewer rows than the forward run. Do not run a full-history backfill unthrottled. Safer fallback: verify idempotency, backfill one partition and compare to a forward run, then batch with throttling while the forward pipeline keeps running.
